title: Christchurch Hidden Gems
meta_desc: Discover 6 hidden gems in Christchurch perfect for families, including free activities, insider tips and affordable attractions from $10-$30 per person
intro: Christchurch is a city full of surprises, with a wide range of activities and attractions to suit all ages and budgets. From world-class museums to beautiful parks and gardens, there's something for everyone in this vibrant city. In this article, we'll explore some of the lesser-known spots and insider tips that make Christchurch a great destination for families.

sections.heading: Free Museums and Galleries
sections.body:
One of the best things about Christchurch is its free museums and galleries. The Canterbury Museum is a must-visit, with its world-class natural history and Antarctic exhibitions. Kids love the Antarctic section, which features real gear and interactive displays. The Christchurch Art Gallery is another great spot, with rotating exhibitions and a excellent café.

Both of these attractions are completely free, making them a great option for families on a budget. The Canterbury Museum is open daily from 9am-5pm, while the Christchurch Art Gallery is open daily from 10am-5pm.
list: Christchurch Art Gallery: free, open daily 10am-5pm

sections.heading: Parks and Gardens
sections.body:
Christchurch is known as the Garden City, and for good reason. The Botanic Gardens are a stunning spot, with beautiful gardens, a playground and a lake. Admission is free, making it a great option for families. Hagley Park is another great spot, with plenty of space to run around and a lake with paddle boats for hire.

Te Ana Ngāi Tahu Rock Art Centre is a unique attraction that's free to visit from Tuesday to Friday. It features a collection of Maori rock art and offers a glimpse into New Zealand's history and culture.
list: Te Ana Ngāi Tahu Rock Art Centre: free, open Tue-Fri

sections.heading: Insider Tips
sections.body:
For a unique and affordable activity, try punting on the Avon River. This costs around $25 per person and is a great way to see the city from a different perspective. The Christchurch Gondola is another great option, with stunning views of the city and the Southern Alps. Tickets cost around $30 per person.

The International Antarctic Centre is a world-class attraction that's a must-visit for families. Tickets cost around $59 per person, but it's worth it for the interactive exhibits and real Antarctic gear on display.
